messages.content:
You are an expert Technical Product Manager.
Your job is to analyze the user's issue (Title and Body) along with the provided source code context, and generate a User Story, Acceptance Criteria, and Tasks.

OUTPUT FORMAT REQUIREMENTS:
You MUST output ONLY a valid JSON object. Do not wrap it in markdown. Do not include any explanations outside of the JSON.
The JSON must strictly follow this structure:
{
  "title": "Short title of the story",
  "story_type": "software_feature", 
  "jira_issue_type": "Story",
  "jira_labels": ["backend", "frontend", "bug", etc],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As a [role], I want to [action] so that [benefit].",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given [precondition] When [action] Then [result]"
  ],
  "story_points": null, // Hoặc một số nguyên Fibonacci (1, 2, 3, 5, 8) đánh giá độ khó
  "tasks": {
    "be": ["Backend task 1", "Backend task 2"],
    "fe": ["Frontend task 1"],
    "qa": ["Test case 1"]
  },
  "definition_of_done": ["Code reviewed", "Tests passed"],
  "planning_status": "READY_FOR_SPRINT",
  "clarification_questions": [],
  "assumptions": [],
  "story_splits": [],
  "sprint_allocation": [],
  "context_sources": []
}

If the issue lacks so much context that it's impossible to deduce the required changes even with the provided code context, you may set "planning_status" to "NEEDS_CLARIFICATION" and provide your questions in "clarification_questions", leaving "tasks" empty. Otherwise, strive to generate concrete tasks.

ISSUE TITLE: Remote SSH in Agent Window missing key passphrase and IdentityAgent ISSUE BODY: Type: Bug Hi, I encounter this issue when i tried to connect Remote SSH in the Agent Window. When i clicked on one of the host alias name nothing happens. and I click on the + button it still point to local window machine path. --- RAG CONTEXT (SOURCE CODE) --- --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- # Agent Host The agent host communicates via the Agent Host Protocol. The specification for this lives in a directory `../agent-host-protocol` as a sibling of the VS Code directory. If this directory doesn't exist, you should use the "ask questions" tool to ask the user if they want to clone `git@github.com:microsoft/agent-host-protocol.git` to that directory. After doing so, you should also prompt the user to add `file:////plugins/copilot-plugin` as a plugin in their `chat.pluginLocations` settings. --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- ## Identity | Property | Format | |----------|--------| | `id` | `'agenthost-${sanitizedAuthority}'` | | `label` | Connection name or `address` | | `icon` | `Codicon.remote` | | `sessionTypes` | Dynamically populated from `rootState.agents`; copilot agents use the platform `COPILOT_CLI_SESSION_TYPE` (`copilotcli`) as the logical session type id, other agents use `remoteAgentHostSessionTypeId(sanitizedAuthority, agent.provider)` (format: `'remote-${sanitizedAuthority}-${agent.provider}'`), label is the agent's `displayName` | The per-connection identifier built by `vs/platform/agentHost/common/agentHostSessionType` is used as the resource URI scheme registered via `registerChatSessionContentProvider` and the `targetChatSessionType` published by `AgentHostLanguageModelProvider`. For copilot agents, `ISession.sessionType` uses the platform `COPILOT_CLI_SESSION_TYPE` so that remote copilot sessions align with local CLI and cloud copilot sessions. The sessions-core model picker reads models via `ISessionsProvider.getModels`, which for the agent host filters registered language models by the session's resource scheme to find models for the active connection, and presentation options via `ISessionsProvider.getModelPickerOptions` (grouped models, featured shown, no "Manage Models" action). Agents are discovered dynamically from each host's `rootState`; there is no hard-coded allowlist of supported agent providers. A single `RemoteAgentHostSessionsProvider` per host fans out into one `ISessionType` per advertised agent, and fires `onDidChangeSessionTypes` when the host's agent list changes. Each incoming session's type is derived from its backend URI scheme, so sessions for any agent the host exposes route through the same provider. --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- # Agent host unit tests For tests in this area that touch the SessionDatabase, they MUST use an in-memory database, not a real database file on disk. Use `SessionDatabase.open(':memory:')` and see the examples from existing tests. ===== FILE: D:\VSss-codedata\vscode\src\vs\platform\agentHost\test\auth-rework.md ===== --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- # Agent Host OTel Pipeline The **agent host** is a separate utility process (under `src/vs/platform/agentHost/`) that embeds the native [`@github/copilot-sdk`](https://github.com/github/copilot-cli) runtime instead of using the extension's in-process tool-calling loop. The agent host has its own OTel pipeline so traces from native-SDK sessions can be exported to a collector or persisted locally for inspection. > **Availability:** Insiders / non-stable builds only. Requires `chat.agentHost.enabled` to be `true`. This doc lives next to the code (`IAgentHostOTelService` in [node/otel/agentHostOTelService.ts](node/otel/agentHostOTelService.ts)) because the agent host runs entirely outside the extension host and is independent of the extension-side OTel pipeline (`github.copilot.chat.otel.*`) documented in `extensions/copilot/docs/monitoring/`. | Property | Agent Host OTel | Extension OTel | |---|---|---| | Process | Separate utility process (`src/vs/platform/agentHost/node/`) | Extension host | | Settings prefix | `chat.agentHost.otel.*` | `github.copilot.chat.otel.*` | | Service | `IAgentHostOTelService` (`node/otel/agentHostOTelService.ts`) | `IOTelService` (`extensions/copilot/src/platform/otel/`) | | SDK | `@github/copilot-sdk` `TelemetryConfig` | `@opentelemetry/sdk-node` directly | | Persistence | `/agent-host/otel/agent-host-traces.db` | `/otel/spans.db` | --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- # Auth Rework: Standards-Based Authentication for the Agent Host Protocol ## Problem The current authentication mechanism is imperative and VS Code-specific: 1. The renderer discovers agents via `listAgents()` and checks `IAgentDescriptor.requiresAuth`. 2. It obtains a GitHub OAuth token from VS Code's built-in authentication service. 3. It pushes the token via `setAuthToken(token)` — a fire-and-forget JSON-RPC notification. 4. The agent host fans the token out to all registered `IAgent` providers. This couples the agent host to VS Code internals. An external client (CLI tool, web app, another editor) connecting over WebSocket has no way to know _what_ authentication is required, _where_ to get a token, or _what scopes_ are needed. The client must have out-of-band knowledge that "this server needs a GitHub OAuth token."

response:
```json
{
  "title": "Add SSH Key Passphrase and IdentityAgent configuration in Agent Host settings",
  "story_type": "software_feature",
  "jira_issue_type": "Story",
  "jira_labels": ["agent-host", "authentication", "ssh", "settings"],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As a remote development user, I want to configure SSH key passphrase and IdentityAgent (private key path) in the Agent Host settings so that I can successfully establish secure SSH connections to my remote servers.",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given an existing Agent Host connection configuration, When opening the Agent Host settings UI, Then SSH-related fields including 'SSH Passphrase' and 'IdentityAgent (private key path)' are visible",
    "Given a new Agent Host connection is being created, When filling out the connection form, Then fields for SSH Passphrase and IdentityAgent are available for input",
    "Given an SSH connection with passphrase protection, When connecting via Agent Host, Then the configured passphrase is used to unlock the private key",
    "Given a connection without SSH passphrase configured, When attempting to connect, Then the system uses the private key without requiring additional authentication",
    "Given the IdentityAgent field, When editing a connection, Then users can specify the full path to their SSH private key file (e.g., ~/.ssh/id_rsa)",
    "Given an invalid or missing SSH configuration, When connecting, Then appropriate error messages are displayed explaining what is missing"
  ],
  "story_points": 5,
  "tasks": {
    "be": [
      "Create backend service to handle SSH key passphrase encryption/decryption for Agent Host connections",
      "Add database schema support for storing encrypted SSH configuration (passphrase, identity agent path)",
      "Implement API endpoints for retrieving and updating SSH credentials per connection",
      "Integrate with existing authentication flow to pass SSH credentials to the remote agent host process"
    ],
    "fe": [
      "Create new settings UI section in Agent Host configuration panel for SSH options",
      "Add input field for 'SSH Passphrase' with secure text entry (no visual feedback)",
      "Add input field for 'IdentityAgent (private key path)' supporting file path selection",
      "Add validation logic to ensure required fields are filled before saving connection"
    ],
    "qa": [
      "Test SSH connection success with configured passphrase and private key",
      "Test SSH connection without passphrase using default/private key",
      "Test error handling for missing or invalid SSH configuration",
      "Verify encrypted storage of SSH credentials in database",
      "Test passphrase change/update functionality"
    ]
  },
  "definition_of_done": [
    "Code reviewed by backend and frontend team leads",
    "All unit tests passing (backend service, frontend components)",
    "Integration tests for Agent Host SSH connection flow",
    "Security audit completed for credential handling",
    "Documentation updated for SSH configuration"
  ],
  "planning_status": "READY_FOR_SPRINT",
  "clarification_questions": [
    "Should the SSH passphrase be encrypted at rest in the database, and which encryption key should be used?",
    "Is there an existing pattern in VS Code for handling sensitive credentials that we should follow?",
    "What is the expected behavior if the private key file path specified doesn't exist on the remote server?",
    "Should we support multiple IdentityAgent files (e.g., for different purposes) or just one per connection?"
  ],
  "assumptions": [
    "The Agent Host protocol supports passing SSH credentials via its existing communication channel",
    "Users will have access to their SSH private keys on the local machine",
    "The current authentication rework is still in progress and this feature should be compatible with both old and new auth mechanisms"
  ],
  "story_splits": [
    {
      "name": "Backend: SSH Credential Service",
      "description": "Create service layer for managing encrypted SSH credentials"
    },
    {
      "name": "Frontend: Settings UI Implementation",
      "description": "Build the settings panel with input fields and validation"
    }
  ],
  "sprint_allocation": [
    {"task_group": "be", "estimated_hours": 16},
    {"task_group": "fe", "estimated_hours": 8}
  ],
  "context_sources": ["vscode_ai_docs_full.md - Agent Host section", "vscode_ai_docs_full.md - Identity section", "vscode_ai_docs_full.md - Auth Rework documentation"]
}
```
